title: Head of Revenue Operations
location: New York, NY / Tel Aviv (Globally distributed)
description: Finout, a cloud cost management SaaS backed by $85M+ and trusted by brands like The New York Times and Lyft, seeks a Head of Revenue Operations reporting to the CRO. The role is a hands-on leadership position to build and scale the operational backbone of the GTM engine. Requires 7+ years in RevOps, Sales Ops, or Strategy in high-growth B2B SaaS, experience scaling beyond $30M+ ARR, deep Salesforce administration expertise, and collaboration with the Tel Aviv HQ across time zones.
score_reason: Exceptional alignment: Head of Revenue Operations role matches target title perfectly, B2B SaaS fintech company aligns with strong fit verticals, 15+ years exceeds 7+ requirement, and global/Tel Aviv distribution accommodates location preferences.
ai_opener: Omri, I came across Finout's Head of RevOps opening and immediately thought of you—scaling GTM operations at a well-funded fintech SaaS that's already reached $85M+ is exactly the complexity you've built systems to handle. With your deep Salesforce expertise and track record scaling revenue operations through hyper-growth stages, you'd be positioned to build the operational backbone the CRO needs while collaborating seamlessly with the Tel Aviv team across time zones.

title: RevOps Manager, Territory Strategy & Operations
description: Gong, a revenue intelligence platform, seeks a RevOps Manager focused on Territory Strategy & Operations to accelerate revenue growth and seller productivity through data-driven investment strategy and scalable territory design. Responsibilities include maintaining territory mapping in Salesforce/Fullcast, leading market expansion and rep capacity modeling, and partnering with Sales, Strategy, and Finance. Requires 3–5+ years in Revenue Operations or Sales Strategy, deep understanding of B2B SaaS GTM models, strong analytical skills (Excel, SQL, BI tools), and Salesforce proficiency.
score_reason: Exceptional alignment: RevOps Manager role matches target position exactly, territory strategy and Salesforce/Fullcast expertise are core strengths, and B2B SaaS GTM experience directly applicable. Only minor gap: hybrid model requires location confirmation, but appears geographically feasible.
ai_opener: Omri, I came across the RevOps Manager role at Gong focused on Territory Strategy & Operations—your 15+ years scaling B2B SaaS GTM and deep Salesforce expertise seems like a natural fit for their market expansion and capacity modeling challenges. Given Gong's focus on revenue intelligence and data-driven seller productivity, your track record optimizing CAC/LTV and pipeline forecasting could directly impact their growth trajectory. Would be worth a conversation to explore how your approach to territory design and rep capacity scaling aligns with their scaling priorities.

title: Senior Revenue Operations Manager
location: Remote (US or Canada; Eastern time zone preferred)
description: IntellaQuest (ASG) is bringing in its first RevOps professional to serve as the strategic backbone of its GTM engine. This is a foundational, player-coach role reporting to the C-suite, architecting systems, processes, and insights that drive predictable revenue across Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success. Ideal for someone who thrives in 0-to-1 environments. Requires GTM/RevOps experience in a mid-market or lower-enterprise sales motion ($25k–$75k ACV), $5M–$30M ARR companies, and expertise with HubSpot, Salesforce, and Gong. Compensation $130k–$140k base.
score_reason: Exceptional match: Omri's 15+ years B2B SaaS expertise, RevOps/GTM focus, and proficiency with HubSpot/Salesforce align perfectly with IntellaQuest's foundational RevOps role. The 0-to-1 player-coach dynamic in mid-market ($5M–$30M ARR) is ideal for his seniority and architectural thinking.
ai_opener: I noticed IntellaQuest is bringing in its first RevOps professional to architect the GTM engine—this is exactly the foundational challenge I've thrived in throughout my career. With 15+ years building predictable revenue systems across Sales, Marketing, and CS in B2B SaaS, and deep hands-on expertise in HubSpot and Salesforce at the $5M–$30M ARR scale, I'm confident I can accelerate your path to scalable, data-driven growth.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Conveo is an AI research platform enabling fast, affordable, high-quality consumer/B2B research, used by global brands like Unilever, Google, and Orange. As the company scales rapidly, they need a GTM Engineer who can code, ship, and iterate on internal tools and automations to make the GTM team highly effective. The role involves replacing manual workflows with purpose-built apps, pipelines, and AI-powered workflows—lead scoring models, attribution pipelines, onboarding flows, and AI agents that eliminate manual work.
score_reason: Strong GTM Engineer alignment with RevOps background and AI-native GTM expertise; however, the role's heavy emphasis on coding/shipping may require verification that Omri's technical depth matches the implementation expectations rather than strategy/ops focus.
ai_opener: Omri, I came across Conveo's GTM Engineer role and thought of you immediately—your 15+ years building scalable revenue operations in B2B SaaS aligns perfectly with what they're building. They're scaling fast (Unilever, Google as customers) and need someone who can architect lead scoring models, attribution pipelines, and AI workflows to eliminate manual work. Your background replacing manual processes with automation in RevOps is exactly the operational thinking they need to support their GTM team.

title: GTM Engineer
description: FacilityOS is a B2B SaaS company helping mid-to-large enterprises stay safe, compliant, and operationally sharp through its facility management platform. They are building their first AI Workforce—a collection of intelligent agents that help Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success work smarter. As GTM Engineer, you'll build, configure, and continuously improve these agents, working at the intersection of data, automation, and human workflows, designing how the AI systems connect, run, learn, and improve over time.
score_reason: Strong alignment with GTM Engineer role, B2B SaaS, AI-native platform, and RevOps automation expertise. Remote global flexibility matches perfectly; only minor gap is that this is more AI/agent-building focused than traditional pipeline forecasting.
ai_opener: FacilityOS's AI Workforce approach resonates deeply—I've spent 15+ years optimizing GTM workflows in B2B SaaS, and the shift from manual ops to intelligent agents is exactly where the category is heading. Your vision of AI agents learning from Sales, Marketing, and CS workflows aligns with how I've always thought about RevOps: removing friction at scale and letting data drive decisions.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Pylon seeks a GTM Engineer to test, implement, and optimize an AI outbound solution from setup through ongoing performance tuning. Responsibilities include building automated outbound sequences with high deliverability, owning email marketing infrastructure (domain health, warming, deliverability), creating data pipelines between marketing tools, CRM, and sales tooling, collaborating with RevOps on lead scoring and routing, and shipping growth experiments. Requires 3+ years in marketing operations or GTM/growth engineering at B2B SaaS companies, with hands-on experience in automated outbound and strong technical skills (APIs, webhooks).
score_reason: Strong alignment with Omri's RevOps/GTM expertise, AI-native GTM interest, and technical infrastructure skills (pipelines, integrations, deliverability). The role is a perfect fit for a senior executive looking to own full-stack GTM operations, though it may feel slightly IC-focused versus traditional head-of-function positioning.
ai_opener: Hi, I came across Pylon's GTM Engineer role and it immediately resonated—building automated outbound sequences with deliverability at scale is exactly the intersection of RevOps rigor and growth velocity that I've spent 15 years optimizing in B2B SaaS. I've owned email infrastructure, data pipelines between marketing/CRM/sales tools, and lead scoring models across multiple high-growth fintech and SaaS companies. I'm particularly drawn to how you're combining AI-native GTM with hands-on technical implementation—that's the future of revenue operations.

title: GTM Engineer (Remote)

As we scale our SDR organization, we need someone who can own the technical setup and optimization of our platform while serving as the co-pilot between Sales and product. title: Lead Marketing & Growth Operations Analyst
description: Tebra, a healthcare SaaS company, seeks a Lead Marketing & Growth Operations Analyst to connect data to growth strategy and translate insights into operational levers and measurable revenue impact. Requires 5–7+ years in Marketing Analytics, Growth Analytics, Revenue Operations, or GTM Analytics within a SaaS or high-growth environment, deep understanding of marketing funnels, lifecycle stages, attribution models, and campaign performance, strong SQL skills, experience with modern data stacks (Snowflake, dbt, BI tools), and the ability to influence decisions and partner with Marketing, Growth, and Sales stakeholders.
score_reason: Strong fit on RevOps/GTM expertise, data analytics, and SaaS background, but role emphasizes marketing analytics and modern data stack (SQL/dbt/Snowflake) where depth is unclear. Healthcare SaaS is adjacent to Omri's fintech/B2B focus. Senior executive may see this as lateral or step-down depending on scope.
ai_opener: I've spent 15+ years building GTM infrastructure and revenue operations at scale in B2B SaaS—most recently connecting data-driven insights to measurable pipeline and revenue impact. Tebra's focus on translating marketing analytics into operational levers aligns with how I've approached growth strategy, and I'm particularly drawn to how you're using modern data stacks to drive accountability across marketing and sales.

title: Head of Sales and Marketing (B2B SaaS)
description: saas.group, a fully remote SaaS portfolio company with 350+ colleagues across 50+ countries and 23 brands, seeks a Head of Sales & Marketing to lead all commercial activities for one of its brands. You'll own the full strategy across acquisition, activation, closing, retention, and expansion, build a modern sales process, and drive multi-channel pipeline generation. Requires 7–10+ years of commercial experience across B2B SaaS, a track record scaling revenue engines in SMB/mid-market, deep understanding of SaaS metrics (ARR/MRR, CAC, LTV, NDR), and strong leadership skills.
score_reason: Strong alignment on B2B SaaS expertise, SaaS metrics mastery, and remote flexibility. However, this is a broader Head of Sales & Marketing role rather than pure RevOps/GTM operations focus—Omri's deepest strength is in building revenue infrastructure and forecasting, not leading sales teams or owning marketing strategy end-to-end.
ai_opener:
Hi [Hiring Manager],

I've been following saas.group's portfolio approach to scaling multiple B2B SaaS brands globally. With 15+ years scaling revenue operations and GTM engines in SaaS—including deep expertise in pipeline architecture, SaaS unit economics (CAC/LTV/NDR), and Salesforce/HubSpot-driven sales processes—I'd be interested in exploring how to help one of your brands build a modern, metrics-driven commercial engine. I'm particularly drawn to fully-remote operations where operational rigor compounds across a global team.

title: Senior Vice President of Global Marketing

ai_opener: Omri—I came across Cloudinary's SVP Marketing opening and thought of you immediately given your 15+ years driving predictable revenue in B2B SaaS. While your expertise sits in the RevOps/GTM operations side rather than brand/demand gen leadership, your deep understanding of pipeline mechanics, Sales alignment, and funnel analytics would make you a phenomenal sounding board or strategic operations partner for whoever lands this role—especially as they scale enterprise demand generation and prove ROI.
